question_text: A magnetic force acts between a magnet and a piece of iron even when they are not touching. Classify this force and explain why it is classified in this way.
correct_answer: This is a non-contact force because the magnetic force acts over a distance without the objects being in physical contact with one another.
explanation: Non-contact forces are forces that can act between objects even when they are not touching. Magnetic forces, gravitational forces, and electrostatic forces are all non-contact forces. This is different from contact forces like friction, which require surfaces to be in direct physical contact.
